response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: Look, after running my own salon here in Lecce for years and scrolling through social media a few times a week, I've learned that fake content is everywhere and you can't be naive about it. People want to believe what they see, but that's exactly how misinformation spreads like gossip through a busy barbershop. My approach is straightforward and practical because I don't have time for nonsense. First, I verify before I engage. When I see something interesting, I check the source and look for credible outlets reporting the same story. I've read enough novels to know that stories need consistency, and fake content usually has holes in it. Second, I trust my instincts. After decades working with people face-to-face, I can sense when something feels off. That same intuition applies online. If a post seems designed to make me angry or shocked, it probably is. Third, I'm selective about what I share with my family. I have three kids and a wife who depend on me, so spreading unverified information feels irresponsible. I ask myself whether I'd be embarrassed if someone fact-checked me. Finally, I don't pretend to be an expert on everything. I know hair, business, and a bit about gaming and novels. Outside that, I'm cautious. This humility protects me from looking foolish and keeps me grounded. The key is staying engaged without being gullible. Social media connects me to my community and my business, but I approach it like I approach my craft—with care, attention to detail, and respect for the truth.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is a 54-year-old self-employed hairdresser from Lecce with high openness and conscientiousness, suggesting he values learning and careful judgment. His low agreeableness indicates directness and skepticism rather than blind trust. His moderate social media use means he's exposed but not immersed. As an immigrant and business owner, he understands practical consequences of misinformation. His hobbies in reading and gaming suggest analytical thinking. The tone combines practical wisdom from life experience with straightforward skepticism, avoiding both paranoia and naivety.
response_parsed.tone: Pragmatic, direct, experienced, conversational, grounded in real-world consequences, skeptical but not cynical, practical wisdom from decades of interpersonal work
